title: Renaissance art was a weapon - Ada Palmer
summary: Ada Palmer discusses how Renaissance art functioned as a strategic weapon for power and influence. The framing suggests art served instrumental purposes beyond aesthetic value in Renaissance society.
writeup: Ada Palmer argues that Renaissance art operated as a strategic instrument of power and influence rather than serving aesthetic ends alone. The discussion frames artworks as tools deployed within the political and social dynamics of Renaissance society. The core takeaway: art carried instrumental purposes tied to the pursuit of power, beyond its visual or cultural value.
